root.narration: You are in the middle of a long rally. The ball keeps coming back and you are getting tired. Your opponent is waiting for you to make a mistake. What is your plan?

choices.text: Hit the ball high over the net with good depth to the middle of the court and stay patient until you get a short ball
Try to end the rally right now by hitting the ball as hard as you can to the corner
Hit the ball softly and short so you can catch your breath

outcome.headline: Smart rally play!
outcome.explanation: By hitting deep and being patient, you kept your opponent pinned behind the baseline. Eventually they hit a short ball and you were ready to attack it for a winner.
outcome.whatToRemember: In a rally, consistency wins. Hit the ball with good height over the net and aim deep. Wait for a short ball before attacking.

keyTerms.definition: When both players keep hitting the ball back and forth over the net. Staying patient and consistent during rallies is one of the most important skills in tennis

outcome.headline: Risky shot missed!
outcome.explanation: Going for a big corner shot without a setup is a gamble. This time the ball clipped the net and went out. You gave away a point you could have won by being patient.
outcome.whatToRemember: Do not go for a winner from behind the baseline unless you have a short ball to attack. Build the point first.

outcome.headline: You gave your opponent an easy ball!
outcome.explanation: Hitting the ball short and soft is like giving your opponent a present. They stepped inside the baseline and hit an easy winner into the open court.
outcome.whatToRemember: Never hit short on purpose during a rally. Even when tired, aim deep to keep your opponent back.
